title: A book I borrowed and forgot to return
summary: Describe a book you borrowed from someone and never returned, and explain why.
topic: A book I borrowed and forgot to return
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a book you borrowed from a friend or a library and forgot to return. Describe what the book was about and when you borrowed it. Use past simple. Explain why you did not return it and how you feel about it now.
hintStarters: A few years ago, I borrowed a book from … and I never gave it back…
The book was about … and I remember thinking I would only keep it for…
Every time I see it on my shelf, I think about…

hintVocab.meaningVi: một cuốn sách cũ, đã qua sử dụng
cứ định làm mà chưa làm

sampleText: About five years ago, I borrowed a book from my friend Priya when we were both living in Melbourne. The book was a novel called 'The Kite Runner' and she said it was one of her favourites. She lent me her personal copy, which was an old, worn paperback with a soft red cover. I promised to return it in two weeks. I started reading it that same night and finished it in four days. I wanted to return it but then I moved to a different neighbourhood and Priya and I slowly lost touch with each other. The book stayed on my shelf. Every time I looked at it, I thought about returning it, but I kept meaning to and never did. Three years later, I found Priya on social media and sent her a message. I told her I still had the book and I was sorry. She laughed and said she had forgotten about it completely. She told me to keep it because she had bought a new copy. I still have the book on my shelf today. I feel a little guilty when I see it, but I also remember how much I loved reading it that first week.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cho tôi mượn cuốn sách cá nhân của cô ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: an old, worn paperback with a soft red cover
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một cuốn sách bìa mềm cũ, đã sờn với bìa màu đỏ nhạt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: promised to return it in two weeks
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hứa sẽ trả lại sau hai tuần

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The book stayed on my shelf
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Cuốn sách ở lại trên kệ của tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I kept meaning to and never did
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi cứ định làm nhưng chưa bao giờ làm
tìm thấy Priya trên mạng xã hội

sampleAnnotations.phrase: she had forgotten about it completely
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cô ấy đã hoàn toàn quên mất chuyện đó
Tôi cảm thấy hơi có lỗi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: how much I loved reading it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi đã yêu thích việc đọc nó đến thế nào

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a sequence of completed actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple cho một chuỗi hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ: 'I started reading it ... and finished it in four days.' Mỗi động từ ở past simple đánh dấu một bước tiếp theo trong câu chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple for a sequence of completed past actions: 'I started reading it ... and finished it in four days.' Each past simple verb marks the next step in the story.

sampleTranslationVi: Khoảng năm năm trước, tôi mượn một cuốn sách từ bạn tôi là Priya khi chúng tôi đang sống ở Melbourne. Cuốn sách là tiểu thuyết 'The Kite Runner' và cô ấy nói đó là một trong những cuốn yêu thích của mình. Cô ấy cho tôi mượn cuốn sách cá nhân của mình, là một cuốn bìa mềm cũ, đã sờn với bìa màu đỏ nhạt. Tôi hứa sẽ trả lại sau hai tuần. Tôi bắt đầu đọc ngay tối hôm đó và đọc xong trong bốn ngày. Tôi muốn trả lại nhưng sau đó tôi chuyển đến khu vực khác và Priya và tôi dần mất liên lạc với nhau. Cuốn sách ở lại trên kệ của tôi. Mỗi lần nhìn thấy nó, tôi nghĩ đến việc trả lại, nhưng cứ định làm mà chưa bao giờ làm. Ba năm sau, tôi tìm thấy Priya trên mạng xã hội và nhắn tin cho cô ấy. Tôi nói tôi vẫn còn cuốn sách và xin lỗi. Cô ấy cười và nói đã hoàn toàn quên mất chuyện đó. Cô bảo tôi cứ giữ lấy vì cô đã mua cuốn mới. Cuốn sách vẫn còn trên kệ của tôi hôm nay. Tôi cảm thấy hơi có lỗi khi nhìn thấy nó, nhưng tôi cũng nhớ mình đã yêu thích việc đọc nó trong tuần đầu tiên đó như thế nào.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who did the writer borrow the book from?
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer borrowed the book from a friend called Priya.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the book look like?
options: An old, worn paperback with a soft red cover
A new hardback with a blue cover
A thick book with a green cover
A small book with no cover
mcQuestions.explanation: The text describes it as 'an old, worn paperback with a soft red cover.'
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the physical description of the book.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why did the writer not return the book?
options: The writer moved and lost touch with Priya
Priya said to keep it from the start
The writer forgot where Priya lived
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer moved to a different neighbourhood and Priya and the writer lost touch.
mcQuestions.hint: What happened after the writer moved?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'She ___ me her personal copy.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Lend' means to give something to someone temporarily. 'Lent' is the past simple form.
mcQuestions.hint: Who gave the book to whom? 'Lend' = give temporarily; 'borrow' = take temporarily.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'Lost touch with' means you stopped communicating or having contact with someone.
mcQuestions.hint: It means you are no longer in contact.

mcQuestions.prompt: How did Priya react when the writer contacted her?
options: She laughed and said she had forgotten about the book
She was angry and asked for the book back
She said she needed the book urgently
mcQuestions.explanation: Priya laughed and said she had forgotten about it completely.
mcQuestions.hint: What was Priya's reaction to the message?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple sequence?
options: I started reading it that same night and finished it in four days.
I was starting reading it that same night and finished it in four days.
I started reading it that same night and was finishing it in four days.
I start reading it that same night and finish it in four days.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs in a completed sequence use past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: A sequence of completed past actions uses past simple for each verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'kept meaning to' mean in 'I kept meaning to and never did'?
options: Intended to do something repeatedly but never did
Wanted to but was too busy
Tried to do it but failed
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Keep meaning to do something' means you have an ongoing intention but you never follow through.
mcQuestions.hint: It means having an intention that you kept delaying.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct word: 'A library book that is not returned on time is ___.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Overdue' means past the expected return date.
mcQuestions.hint: The prefix 'over-' here means 'past the time'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct relative clause: 'which was an old, worn paperback ___ a soft red cover.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'With' is used to describe a physical feature or characteristic of something.
mcQuestions.hint: To describe features of an object, use 'with'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker introduces the reason for keeping the book?
options: because she had bought a new copy
although she had bought a new copy
so she had bought a new copy
while she had bought a new copy
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' introduces the reason: Priya said keep it because she had a new copy.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever borrowed something and not returned it? Do you think borrowing things from friends can sometimes cause problems in a friendship? Why or why not?
